summary: The report is technically rigorous, mathematically consistent, and strongly supported by quantitative evidence from both theory and Monte Carlo simulation. It satisfies the required structure, includes all requested formulas/tables/figure references, and provides a credible assessment of both strengths and limitations. The main risk is that some comparative conclusions (especially cyclostationary behavior) are configuration-specific.
technical_strengths: Exact Neyman-Pearson threshold design with correct chi-square/gamma modeling and consistent normalization
Excellent theory-simulation agreement with maximum absolute Pd gap of 0.0010993, far below the 0.02 target
Comprehensive evaluation including fixed-Pfa curves, ROC/AUC, complexity/runtime, and robustness stress testing
technical_risks: Energy detector performance is highly sensitive to noise-power mismatch, with extreme Pfa distortion under +1 dB mismatch
Cyclostationary baseline appears under-tuned for the given snapshot and parameter settings, limiting fairness of broad method conclusions
Results are validated under ideal AWGN and Gaussian signal assumptions; generalization to colored/interference-rich channels is uncertain

main_concerns: Robustness mismatch outputs include extreme values (0 and 0.91709 Pfa), which are plausible but highlight deployment fragility
Duplicate figure assets represent repeated renderings rather than distinct new analyses

actionable_notes: Add confidence intervals for empirical Pfa and Pd to quantify Monte Carlo uncertainty explicitly
Include a fairness-tuned cyclostationary configuration (optimized alpha/tau grid and threshold calibration) for stronger comparative interpretation
Extend validation to colored noise or adjacent-channel interference to test model robustness beyond ideal AWGN
